zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn於1142年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，她的父親William d’Aubigny9歲，母親Matilda de Saint-Hilaire10歲。她於1165年之前與William de Graham結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1216年2月6日在英國蘇格蘭培爾享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可列維斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn于1142年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾时，她的父亲William d’Aubigny9岁，母亲Matilda de Saint-Hilaire10岁。她于1165年之前与William de Graham结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1216年2月6日在英国苏格兰培爾享年74岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素可列維斯。

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Strathearn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Strathearn in Perthshire. The placename means ‘the valley of the river Earn’, from Gaelic srath ‘valley’ + the river name Earn.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn was born in 1142, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, William d’Aubigny, was 9 and her mother, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, was 10. She married William de Graham before 1165. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 6 February 1216, in Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in Lewes, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn est née en 1142, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, William d’Aubigny, avait 9 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, avait 10 ans. Elle a épousé William de Graham avant 1165. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 6 février 1216, à Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn 1142 in Arundel, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William d’Aubigny 9 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Saint-Hilaire 10. Sie hat vor 1165 William de Graham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 6. Februar 1216 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Lewes, Sussex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearnが1142年にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父William d’Aubignyは9歳，母Matilda de Saint-Hilaireは10歳であった。1165年以前にWilliam de Grahamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1216年2月6日にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススセックスルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn(이)가 1142년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William d’Aubigny(은)는 9세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Saint-Hilaire(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 1165년 이전 William de Graham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1216년 2월 6일 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 루스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn nasceu em 1142, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William d’Aubigny, tinha 9 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, tinha 10 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Graham antes 1165. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda d’Aubigny, Countess of Strathearn родилась в 1142 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени William d’Aubigny было 9 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, 10. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Graham перед 1165 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 6 февраля 1216 г. в местности пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности львесь, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con William de Graham antes de 1165. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 6 de febrero de 1216, en Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultada en Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
